what_it_does: React (no-JSX-transform, React.createElement + JSX-via-Babel) components that render the AtomiCity Atlas browser: a collapsible tree rail (AcTree/AcTreeNode) on the left and a detail pane (AcDetail/AcBody plus per-kind leaf renderers) on the right, both driven entirely by the window.ATLAS projection model. The detail pane dispatches on node.kind to render tokens, cards (iframed specimens), components (live-mounted with an error boundary), templates (iframed), themes, fonts, AI entries (with lineage), Types, runtimes, serializers, and staged changes; every detail view ends with a 'What Vi can do here' chip row sourced from window.VI_BRAIN.suggestions. Also defines EnvCheck, a live read of CV_HOST/CV_SCAN capabilities showing what is autonomous now vs only on export. Exposes window.AcTree, window.AcDetail, window.EnvCheck.
observations: Header comment states both panes render purely from the ATLAS model so they 'can't drift from the system' and that 'the agent and the browser are welded'.
Foundations is treated as one workbench page: token-group/token-file children are hidden in the tree (hideKids) and clicking a token node opens window.AcFoundations rather than deep-drilling.
AcBody routes special sections (foundations, home, ingest, explore, voice) to dedicated window.Ac* surfaces, falls back to a tile grid for sections/groups, then to per-kind leaf renderers.
AcMount is a class component with componentDidCatch error boundary; if a component needs props to render standalone it degrades to an explanatory message pointing to the specimen card.
AcCard and AcTemplate render the real artifact via <iframe src='../'+path> with viewport-derived heights.
AcToken always asserts 'This is its single home' and tells the user to reference var(--name) — reinforcing the single-source doctrine in the UI copy itself.
AcAiEntry calls window.CV_AI.lineage(e.id) and renders the inheritance chain; AcChange renders the serialized source diff in a <pre>.
Aliased hook imports (useState_at etc.) to avoid collisions in the shared global React bundle.

EnvCheck derives capability rows (read/write/list/exec/scan) from CV_HOST.describe().capabilities with no hand-written status; copy explains export-with-file-access unlocks autonomous write/scan/build.
Detail pane hard-depends on window.VI_BRAIN, window.ATLAS, and (for AI lineage) window.CV_AI being present.
standouts: Every node's detail ends with agent affordances ('What Vi can do here'), structurally welding the browser to the AI rather than treating them as separate panes.
Live component mounting with an error-boundary fallback to the specimen card, so even un-mountable components stay inspectable.
EnvCheck makes the runtime's own capability surface (read/write/scan/exec) visible and honest about what only works on export.
conventions: Pure projection of the ATLAS model — no hand-written navigation or per-page status, matching the project's 'interface is a projection of the registries' rule.
window.Ac* global component registration; React.createElement for cross-component composition with globals.
concerns: AcBody and AcDetail assume window.VI_BRAIN / window.ATLAS / window.CV_AI exist with no guards in some paths (e.g. window.VI_BRAIN.suggestions called unconditionally in AcDetail); a missing global would throw rather than degrade — file-local observation only.
notes: The Atlas browser UI — a tree + kind-dispatched detail pane rendered entirely from window.ATLAS, where every inspected thing also shows its agent actions; pair with atlas-model.js (the model) and the Ac* surface components it delegates to.
questions: What is window.VI_BRAIN.suggestions and where is it defined — it supplies the per-node agent actions but is not in this file?
Where do AcFoundations/AcHome/AcIngest/AcExplore/AcVoice live, and what is the AcKit.Tile / CvIcon / ViShape component set this depends on?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — the header promises a tree + detail pane rendered purely from ATLAS that always shows what Vi can do, and the code implements exactly that with kind-based dispatch and the trailing agent-action chips.
apparent_themes: Agent and interface welded (inspect = also act)
Single-source doctrine surfaced in UI copy
intention_signals: Trying to make exploring the design system and acting on it the same act — every entity is rendered live from its source of truth and immediately offers the AI moves available on it, so the human and Vi operate on one shared model.
novelty: A registry-projected explorer whose every detail view both renders the real artifact (live mount or iframe) and ends with context-resolved agent actions, plus a self-reporting environment-capability widget.
contribution: Contributes the human-facing rendering and interaction layer over the ATLAS projection: the tree, the kind-dispatched detail renderers (live component mount, iframed cards/templates, token/AI/type/change views), and the EnvCheck capability disclosure.
summary_for_embedding: React components rendering the AtomiCity Atlas browser — a collapsible tree and a kind-dispatched detail pane (tokens, cards, live-mounted components, templates, AI entries, types, staged changes) plus an EnvCheck widget, all driven purely by the window.ATLAS projection and ending every view with context-resolved 'what Vi can do here' actions.
intention_for_embedding: To let a person browse and act on the design system through one projection-driven UI where inspecting any entity also exposes the AI moves available on it.
